sections.context: Records today are anonymous beyond the git commit author. At 50-IC scale this makes contradictions, stale records, and review queues unroutable — there is no one to ping when records conflict, anchors break, or shelf life expires. R-06 is also a hard prerequisite for R-12 (contradiction detection), which has nowhere to send the resolution without an owner. Sequencing list flags this as Next now that R-04 has shipped.
sections.approach: Add `owner` (string, e.g. `@user` or `@team`) and `status` (`draft|active|deprecated`) as built-in optional record fields, then introduce a four-link owner-resolution chain evaluated at `ml record` write time: explicit `--owner` flag → CODEOWNERS lookup against the record's file_anchors/dir_anchors → per-domain `default_owner` → git author fallback. Per-domain `require_owner: true` opts a domain into hard validation (matches the existing `required_fields` enforcement pattern from mulch-3114). Layered on top, ship `ml review` as a read-only command listing records owned by `$USER` that need attention (status=draft, near-expiry, or flagged by doctor). Chosen over alternatives because it reuses the per-domain rules plumbing already shipped, keeps JSONL as the single source of truth, and degrades gracefully when neither CODEOWNERS nor config is configured (git author is always available).

alternatives.name: Single global default_owner only (no resolution chain)
alternatives.rejected_because: Too coarse at org scale — backend records and frontend records have different teams. Per-domain default_owner is the minimum viable granularity.

alternatives.name: Owner stored externally (e.g. .mulch/owners.yaml mapping IDs to handles)
alternatives.rejected_because: Breaks JSONL-as-source-of-truth and creates a second file to keep in sync. Records carry their own owner inline.

alternatives.name: Resolve owner only at read time (e.g. during prime/review)
alternatives.rejected_because: CODEOWNERS changes over time; resolving lazily means the same record reports different owners on different days. Stamp once at write time; let `ml doctor` recompute on demand if needed.

alternatives.name: Skip CODEOWNERS, use only --owner + default_owner + git author
alternatives.rejected_because: CODEOWNERS is already the canonical org ownership map for files. Bypassing it forces orgs to duplicate that mapping in mulch.config.yaml.

steps.title: Schema: add owner and status as built-in optional record fields
steps.description: Add `owner: string` and `status: 'draft' | 'active' | 'deprecated'` (default `active`) to the base record schema in src/schemas/record.ts and the generic AJV factory. Both are optional and additive — existing records without these fields remain valid. Update src/utils/expertise.ts dedup logic to ignore status when computing dedup keys (so flipping draft → active does not create a duplicate). Add tests covering round-trip read/write/prime.

steps.title: Config: add domains.<name>.require_owner and domains.<name>.default_owner
steps.description: Extend DomainConfig in src/utils/config.ts with optional `require_owner: boolean` (default false) and `default_owner: string`. Validate at config load time: require_owner only meaningful if owner is allowed by allowed_types, default_owner must look like a handle (`@<name>`). Update mulch.config.yaml init wizard examples. Tests in test/utils/config.test.ts.

steps.title: CODEOWNERS parser utility
steps.description: Add src/utils/codeowners.ts implementing GitHub's CODEOWNERS format: blank lines and `#` comments ignored, lines are `<glob> <owner1> <owner2>...`, last matching pattern wins. Honor canonical lookup paths in order: `CODEOWNERS`, `.github/CODEOWNERS`, `docs/CODEOWNERS`. Read-only utility — no caching beyond a single load per command invocation. Export `resolveOwners(filePath: string, repoRoot: string): string[]`. Tests cover globs, comments, blanks, missing files, multi-owner lines.

steps.title: Owner resolution chain at ml record write time
steps.description: In src/commands/record.ts, after schema validation and before file write: if no --owner flag provided, walk the chain — CODEOWNERS lookup against each file_anchor/dir_anchor (first match wins) → domain.default_owner → git author email. Stamp the result onto the record. If domain.require_owner is true and the chain produces no result (impossible with git author fallback unless we are not in a git repo), exit non-zero with a clear hint pointing at --owner / CODEOWNERS / default_owner. Tests in test/commands/record.test.ts.

steps.title: ml review command: list records owned by $USER needing attention
steps.description: New src/commands/review.ts (read-only, no locking needed). Default scope: records where owner matches `$USER` (resolved from git config user.email or --owner override) AND any of: status=draft, classification=tactical with age > 10 days, classification=observational with age > 25 days, or doctor reports flagged anchors/domain violations. Flags: `--owner <handle>`, `--status <draft|active|deprecated>`, `--json`. Joins the read-only command tier (prime/query/search/status/validate/learn/ready/rank). Tests cover scope, filters, and json shape.

risks: CODEOWNERS parser edge cases — glob patterns differ subtly from gitignore, last-match-wins is the opposite of most lookup tables. Mitigate with a fixture-based test suite mirroring github.com/octokit's CODEOWNERS reference cases.

Existing records have no owner. Two options: (a) leave them unowned and let ml doctor surface the gap, (b) grandfather by stamping git-blame author on first read. Pick (a) — explicit migration is safer than silent backfill, matches how dir_anchors[] and extends: were rolled out.

Owner migration when files move: file_anchors may resolve to a different CODEOWNERS owner after a refactor. Decision: do not auto-recompute on move; ml doctor adds a check that reports drift, leaving the fix to the human owner.
@team handles vs @user handles — no validation in v1; treat as opaque strings. ml doctor can later add a CODEOWNERS-membership check.
Performance: CODEOWNERS lookup runs on every ml record. Parse once per invocation, cache in-process. ml record latency budget is already tight; benchmark against a 1000-line CODEOWNERS file.
Interaction with extends: <builtin>: custom types extending built-ins must inherit the new owner/status fields automatically. Verify in test/utils/registry.test.ts.
acceptance: owner and status round-trip through ml record → JSONL → ml prime without loss; existing records without these fields remain valid (backward compatible).
ml record in a domain with default_owner: '@platform-team' and no --owner flag stamps owner='@platform-team' on the record.
ml record in a domain with require_owner: true and no resolvable owner exits non-zero with a hint that names the missing chain links.
CODEOWNERS lookup correctly resolves a file_anchor or dir_anchor to its owner; multi-owner lines return all handles; missing CODEOWNERS file is non-fatal (chain falls through).
ml review --owner @foo lists records owned by @foo with status=draft or near-expiry classifications; --json emits structured rows with id/domain/type/owner/status/reason.
All 1120 existing tests still pass; new tests cover schema, config, CODEOWNERS parser, resolution chain, and ml review.
ROADMAP.md R-06 entry updated with a Shipped: line citing the seed id and date, mirroring R-01/R-04/R-05a/e/f format.

sections.context: Stress-testing PR #21 (release v0.8.0, 58 commits, 10 features) surfaced 20 distinct findings across two adversarial agents and one migration/E2E agent. Existing 1120-test suite passes; these are gaps the unit tests don't cover. Two are critical data-integrity bugs (silent duplicate-ID writes via ml restore; A↔B supersession cycles archive both records). Several others are HIGH-severity UX failures (hook timeout reports kill but CLI hangs; raw Bun stack traces from config typos; recipe loader accepts non-default exports despite spec). The dogfood branch (now merged) exercises 4 of the 10 features in mulch's own .mulch/ — finding #14 and #15 along the way. Bundling these into a single hardening pass keeps the v0.8.x line stable without re-cutting major releases per fix.
sections.approach: Group the 20 findings into 7 logically cohesive child seeds by code path / failure surface, prioritized P0→P3. Each child seed bundles related fixes that touch overlapping code (e.g. all three recipe loader bugs live in src/commands/setup.ts and src/utils/recipe-discovery.ts) so a single PR / test run / review naturally covers them. Critical fixes (restore dup-ID, supersession cycle) ship together because both are archive-lifecycle data integrity. Polish/UX nits batch into one trailer issue. No cross-step blocking dependencies — each child can land independently as soon as a contributor picks it up.

alternatives.name: File 20 individual issues
alternatives.rejected_because: Too granular; many fixes share files and tests, and 20 P0–P3 issues with no chaining is hard to prioritize at a glance. User explicitly asked to batch.

alternatives.name: Single monolithic 'v0.8.1 hardening' bug with 20 checkboxes
alternatives.rejected_because: Loses sd's per-issue lifecycle (assignment, status, close). Hard to parallelize across contributors.

steps.title: P0: Archive-lifecycle data integrity (ml restore dup-IDs + supersession cycles)
steps.description: Fix #1: ml restore appends without checking live expertise for the same id, silently creating duplicates that doctor's content-based dedup misses (src/utils/archive.ts:116). Fix #2: A→B + B→A circular supersession demotes both records every prune pass; both archive within 3 cycles, no cycle detection. Add pre-restore live-id scan with cross-domain-ambiguity-style error; add DFS cycle skip in supersession demotion. Test cases for both. Both are CRITICAL data-loss / corruption bugs.

steps.title: P1: Hook reliability — timeout actually kills + mutable-events doc/code drift
steps.description: Fix #3: hook with timeout_ms: 500 + sleep 60 reports timeout in error message but CLI hangs the full 60s. Cause: Promise.all over Response(stdout/stderr).text() blocks on pipe EOF, which doesn't fire after SIGKILL of sh because the descendant sleep keeps the pipe open. Fix by awaiting proc.exited first, then reading captured streams with a short fallback, or by using detached spawn. Fix #19: runHooks docstring says pre-record/pre-prime/pre-prune are mutable but only pre-record + pre-prime are in MUTABLE_EVENTS — align doc to code. HIGH severity — undermines documented hook safety contract.

steps.title: P1: Provider recipe loader hardening (throw, named exports, --list shadow)
steps.description: Fix #4: recipe install() that throws (sync) or rejects (async) crashes CLI with raw Bun stack — wrap recipe invocation in src/commands/setup.ts:767 with try/catch + 'Recipe X failed: ...' framing. Fix #5: TS recipe with no default export silently accepted because mod.default ?? mod falls through to module namespace; spec says 'must default-export' — explicit check for default presence. Fix #11: --list shows both .ts and .sh entries with the same name as separate rows with no shadow indicator on the loser; show a single row with a shadow flag matching the existing builtin-shadow pattern. All three live in setup/recipe-discovery code. HIGH severity.

steps.title: P1: Config error surfacing — wrap registry init + harden doctor against minimal configs
steps.description: Fix #6: errors thrown by buildRegistryWithCustomTypes (src/registry/init.ts:32) for disabled_types/aliases/extends/custom_types misconfig bubble as raw Bun stack traces with source excerpts. Wrap at CLI top level with formatted config-error output. Fix #9: ml doctor crashes (TypeError: undefined is not an object) when YAML omits governance or classification_defaults blocks (doctor.ts:176, 491) — defensive defaults or schema-required enforcement at readConfig. Fix #17: custom_types entry without dedup_key AND without extends crashes every command at validateCustomTypeConfig instead of failing config validation cleanly — graceful error matching the rest of the validator's style. HIGH severity — every config typo currently produces frightening output.

steps.title: P2: dir_anchors normalization + project-root containment
steps.description: Fix #8: './src/utils' is not normalized to 'src/utils', so dedup misses and a record can store ['./src/utils', 'src/utils'] as separate entries. Extend normalizeDirAnchor (src/utils/dir-anchors.ts:3) to strip './' prefix in addition to handling '' and '.'. Fix #7: --dir-anchor /etc and --dir-anchor ../../escape are accepted; resolve(root, '/etc') discards the prefix and returns /etc, so existsSync passes — abs paths and traversal bypass project-root scoping. Reject absolute paths; reject paths that resolve outside the project root. Both fixes touch the same util. MEDIUM severity.

steps.title: P2: Custom types polish — name regex docs, field types, alias collisions
steps.description: Fix #14: custom_types name regex ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ rejects hyphens; neither CHANGELOG nor init-scaffold says so. Add a doc note to CHANGELOG + the optional-knobs scaffold comment + improve the registry error to name the constraint. Fix #15: all custom-type Ajv field props typed as { type: 'string' } in src/registry/custom.ts:283-289 — flake_rate: 0.05 fails AJV, must stringify. Allow per-field type declaration in custom_types config (e.g. { flake_rate: { type: 'number' } }) while keeping string the default. Fix #16: alias whose legacy_name matches another type's base field (e.g. [name]) is accepted at init; the rewrite only fires for the owning type, but a misclassified record gets silent field renames — reject at config validation. All three are custom-type registry layer. MEDIUM severity.

steps.title: P3: Polish batch — migration wording, archive parse errors, anchor grace days, rank flag parsing, --quiet semantics
steps.description: Fix #10: 'Migration is automatic for legacy configs' implies on-disk migration of domains: string[] → Record<string, DomainConfig>, but writeConfig is only called from add/delete-domain. Either migrate-on-write across all write commands or update CHANGELOG / docs to clarify the in-memory shim. Fix #13: malformed archive line crashes search --archived / restore with raw 'JSON Parse error' — no file:line pointer. Match the targeted 'Unknown record type at file:line (id=...)' error pattern. Fix #12: grace_days: 0 decays records on the same calendar day they were recorded (uses fractional ageDays > graceDays). Either round to whole days or use >= so 0 means 'don't decay anything within the same calendar day'. Fix #18: ml rank --limit '1xyz' parses as 1, --min-score '1.5xyz' parses as 1.5 — parseInt/parseFloat lenient; reject with clear error. Fix #20: --quiet suppresses the disabled_types deprecation warning, silently writing a deprecated record type — counter-intuitive, document or surface a one-line summary even under --quiet. LOW priority docs/UX cleanup batch.

risks: Critical fixes (#1, #2) need careful test coverage so we don't paper over the symptom. Cycle detection via DFS must handle long chains and self-loops without quadratic blow-up.
Hook timeout fix (#3) interacts with the spawn API surface — naive 'kill harder' approaches can race against fast-exiting hooks. Validate against the existing hooks-integration test suite.
Recipe loader changes (#4–#5) could break already-shipping recipes that rely on the lenient default-export fallback. Audit the example recipes (examples/recipes/) and any known npm package consumers.
Custom types per-field type support (#15) is the largest scope item — risks expanding into a feature creep refactor. Keep it minimal: number, boolean, string only; skip enum/regex/union for v1.
Migration wording fix (#10) is partly docs and partly behavior — pick one and ship; do not ship half-on-disk migration.
acceptance: All 7 child seeds closed; tests added/updated in each child PR.

1120-test suite still passes; new regression tests cover at minimum: ml restore over duplicate live id, A↔B supersession cycle prune pass, hook timeout with subprocess that holds stdout open, recipe with no default export rejected, recipe install() throw produces formatted error, config missing governance block produces formatted doctor output (not crash), dir_anchor normalization for './prefix' and rejection of absolute / traversal paths, custom_types hyphenated name produces named-constraint error, alias collision with foreign-type base field rejected at init.
CHANGELOG.md updated with a [0.8.1] section listing each fix with seed id + one-line summary.
ml doctor on the dogfooded .mulch/ config (now merged) continues to pass after each fix lands.

sections.context: Warren V2 (warren ROADMAP R-10) needs every os-eco tool to publish its config JSON Schema so it can render configuration UIs generically — no per-knob form code per tool. Mulch is the highest-leverage starting point: it has the largest config surface in the ecosystem (~20 knobs across governance, classification_defaults, domains, custom_types, hooks, hook_settings, decay, search, prime, disabled_types, version) and its config is already cleanly TS-typed in src/schemas/config.ts. Shipping `ml config schema` plus atomic per-knob writes (`ml config set` / `unset` / `show`) gives warren a stable contract to render forms against and a safe write path that goes through mulch's existing file-lock + atomic-rename machinery.
sections.approach: Hand-author a JSON Schema for MulchConfig in src/schemas/config-schema.ts (mirroring the existing src/schemas/record-schema.ts convention — exported const, AJV-strict-compatible). Hand-authoring (vs. generating from TS) keeps full control over title/description/default/enum/min/max metadata that warren's UI depends on, and avoids adding a build step to a Bun-no-build-step project. Lock the shape with a golden test: intentional schema changes update the golden, accidental drift fails CI. Add a single `ml config` command in src/commands/config.ts (registered in src/cli.ts following the registerXCommand convention) with four subcommands: `schema`, `show`, `set`, `unset`. Reuse the existing concurrency primitives — withFileLock from src/utils/lock.ts and atomic-rename from the writeExpertiseFile pattern — for every write. YAML-parse user-supplied values (so warren can submit lists/objects/primitives uniformly) and validate the full resulting config against the schema before write so invalid values are rejected with a clear, schema-referencing error.

alternatives.name: Generate JSON Schema from TS interface at build time (ts-json-schema-generator)
alternatives.rejected_because: Adds a build step to a project that runs Bun directly with no dist output. Loses control over the title/description/enum/range metadata warren's UI depends on (TS type comments don't map cleanly to JSON Schema). Hand-authoring + golden test gives equivalent drift protection with less moving machinery.

alternatives.name: Single flat `ml config <op>` command with op as positional
alternatives.rejected_because: Commander's subcommand idiom (matches `sd plan <subcmd>`, `ml setup <provider>`) gives per-subcommand --help, scoped flags, and clearer testability. No upside to the flat shape.

alternatives.name: Append-style writes for list-valued knobs (`ml config set domains.warren.allowed_types '+convention'`)
alternatives.rejected_because: Out of scope for V1 per seed's open question. Replacement-only keeps the YAML-parse path uniform across all knobs; append/remove can land as a follow-up once warren's UX needs it.

steps.title: Author MulchConfig JSON Schema in src/schemas/config-schema.ts
steps.description: Create src/schemas/config-schema.ts exporting MULCH_CONFIG_SCHEMA covering every knob in MulchConfig (src/schemas/config.ts:101-137): version, domains (open map → DomainConfig), governance (max_entries/warn_entries/hard_limit), classification_defaults.shelf_life.{tactical,observational}, prime.default_mode (enum: manifest|full), search.boost_factor, custom_types (open map → CustomTypeConfig with extends/required/optional/dedup_key/id_key/summary/extracts_files/files_field/compact (enum: concat|merge_outcomes|keep_latest|manual)/section_title/aliases), decay.anchor_validity.{threshold (0..1)|grace_days (>=0)|weight}, disabled_types, hooks (HOOK_EVENTS keys → string[]), hook_settings.timeout_ms. Every property must have title + description; defaults from DEFAULT_CONFIG and the DEFAULT_* constants; enums for known unions; minimum/maximum on numeric ranges; additionalProperties:false on closed objects, additionalProperties:<schema> on the open maps (domains, custom_types, hooks). Follow mx-b0403f (always include type:'object' alongside required/properties for AJV strict mode) and mx-5baadf (schema lives in .ts, not .json).

steps.title: Add `ml config` command skeleton with `schema` subcommand
steps.description: Create src/commands/config.ts exporting registerConfigCommand(program) per the mx-60f9f9 add-command guide. Wire a `config` parent command with a `schema` subcommand: `ml config schema [--json]`. JSON mode emits the JSON Schema document straight from MULCH_CONFIG_SCHEMA via JSON.stringify(_, null, 2); non-JSON mode prints a human-readable summary (top-level knobs grouped by section, one line each). Register the command in src/cli.ts alongside the other 26. No write path yet — pure read.

steps.title: Add golden test for `ml config schema --json`
steps.description: Add test/commands/config.test.ts following the no-mocks convention (mx-53d431) — real filesystem temp dirs via mkdtemp, assertions against actual stdout. Snapshot the full `ml config schema --json` output to test/fixtures/config-schema.golden.json. Test asserts deep equality. Document in the test file how to update: re-run with UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 (or equivalent) when the schema legitimately changes.

steps.title:
Add `ml config show [--path

] [--json]` subcommand
steps.description: Add a `show` subcommand to ml config. With no --path, emits the resolved config (DEFAULT_CONFIG-backfilled per mx-3b7876, so a minimal user config still produces full output). With --path <dot-notation>, traverses (e.g. `governance.max_entries`, `domains.warren.allowed_types`, `custom_types.release_decision.required`) and emits only that subtree. --json forces JSON output (default), non-JSON pretty-prints YAML. Read-only — no locking needed. Reuse readConfig from src/utils/config.ts.

steps.title: Add `ml config set <path> <value>` subcommand with atomic write
steps.description: Add a `set` subcommand. Parse <value> as YAML (yaml.load) so warren can submit `'[a, b]'` for lists, `'{key: val}'` for objects, or bare scalars uniformly. Walk the dot-notation path (creating intermediate objects for open maps like domains.<name>, custom_types.<name>, hooks.<event>), set the leaf, then run the resulting full config through Ajv against MULCH_CONFIG_SCHEMA. Reject invalid values with an error that quotes the schema's title/description for the offending field. On success: write through withFileLock(configPath, ...) (src/utils/lock.ts) using a temp-file + rename atomic write that mirrors writeExpertiseFile. Auto-create .mulch/mulch.config.yaml if absent (matches `ml init` UX, per seed's lean-yes). Print a canonical YAML diff (or 'set <path>: <value>' confirmation) on success. Reuse getMulchDir from src/utils/git.ts so worktrees route to the main repo's config (mx-376671 / worktree-aware storage).

steps.title: Add `ml config unset <path>` subcommand
steps.description:
Add an `unset` subcommand. Walks the dot-notation path and deletes the leaf (or the whole nested object if path points to one). Re-runs Ajv validation against the post-delete config — if the delete leaves something required missing, emit a clear error and abort. Same withFileLock + atomic-rename write path as `set`. After delete, the value reverts to whatever DEFAULT_CONFIG / DEFAULT_* constant supplies (verified by `ml config show --path

` returning the default). Refuses to unset top-level required fields (version, domains, governance, classification_defaults).

steps.title: Worktree + concurrency integration tests across all subcommands
steps.description: Add integration tests in test/commands/config.test.ts that: (a) run every subcommand from inside a `git worktree add` checkout and assert writes land in the main repo's .mulch/mulch.config.yaml (covers worktree-aware getMulchDir per mx-376671); (b) launch two concurrent `ml config set` processes against different paths and assert both land without corrupting the YAML (covers withFileLock); (c) run `ml config set` with invalid value (e.g. governance.max_entries: 'not a number') and assert non-zero exit + schema-referencing error; (d) run `ml config set` against an absent .mulch/mulch.config.yaml and assert it auto-creates with the new knob plus DEFAULT_CONFIG backfill. Confirm `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` all green.

risks: Hand-authored JSON Schema drifts from the TS MulchConfig interface as new knobs land — golden test catches output drift but not the upstream-TS-vs-schema drift. Mitigation: doctor check or test that walks Object.keys of DEFAULT_CONFIG and asserts each appears in the schema (and vice versa for required top-level fields).

YAML parsing of <value> has surprising edges: bare `yes`/`no` parse as booleans, `1.0` as number, empty string as null. Warren may submit JSON-stringified values that re-parse unexpectedly. Mitigation: test the common cases (boolean knob, numeric knob, list, object, string-with-special-chars) and document the YAML-input contract in the subcommand --help.

Dot-notation traversal into open maps (domains.<name>, custom_types.<name>) needs to create intermediate objects when absent, but must NOT silently create them for closed-shape paths (e.g. `ml config set governance.typo 5` should error, not insert a new field). Mitigation: walk the schema in parallel with the path; reject when the next segment isn't a known property AND the current node is closed (additionalProperties:false).

Auto-creating .mulch/mulch.config.yaml on `ml config set` may surprise users running from outside a mulch project. Mitigation: only auto-create when getMulchDir() resolves successfully (i.e., we're in a git repo with a .mulch/ in the main worktree); otherwise hard-error pointing to `ml init`.

Schema versioning across mulch releases: warren caches the schema per mulch version. If MulchConfig.version bumps from '1' to '2' with breaking changes, warren needs the new shape. Mitigation (V1): emit the current-version schema unconditionally. Document that warren should key its cache on `mulch --version --json` output.

Concurrent `ml config set` writes against the same key could race. The advisory file lock serializes them, but last-writer-wins is the only safe semantic — no conflict detection. Mitigation: doc this in --help; warren UI should re-fetch via `ml config show` after every write.
acceptance: `ml config schema --json` emits a complete JSON Schema document covering every knob in MulchConfig, with title, description, default (where applicable), enum (for unions), and minimum/maximum (for numeric ranges).
Golden test in test/commands/config.test.ts asserts the full schema shape; accidental drift fails CI, intentional changes update the golden via documented procedure.

`ml config set <path> <yaml-value>` parses the value as YAML, validates the resulting full config against the schema, and atomically writes via withFileLock; invalid values are rejected with an error that references the schema's title/description for the offending field.

`ml config show [--path

] [--json]` reads any knob (or the whole config) and emits stable JSON output suitable for warren consumption.

`ml config unset ` removes the knob from disk and `ml config show --path

` subsequently returns the schema default.
All four subcommands work correctly from inside a `git worktree add` checkout (writes land in the main repo's .mulch/mulch.config.yaml).
`bun test`, `bun run lint`, and `bun run typecheck` all pass with no new warnings.

sections.context: Warren V2 (ROADMAP R-11) wants to inject mulch-primed expertise directly into the system prompt sent to coding agents at run-spawn time. The flow shells `ml prime --domain X --files F --budget N --format plain` and concatenates the output into the role's system prompt. For a UI preview, warren also wants `--dry-run` that returns metadata (record IDs, types, domains, token counts, budget usage) without rendering content. `--format plain` already exists in mulch (global flag at src/cli.ts:120; formatDomainExpertisePlain / formatPrimeOutputPlain / formatPrimeManifestPlain in src/utils/format.ts), but no audit has been done against the spawn-injection contract, and prime.ts unconditionally appends the Session Close Protocol reminder (src/commands/prime.ts:365 via getSessionEndReminder) which would pollute a system prompt. `--dry-run` does not exist on prime today.
sections.approach: Two narrow, additive changes to src/commands/prime.ts and src/utils/format.ts, plus an audit pass on the existing plain formatters. (1) Audit the existing `formatDomainExpertisePlain` / `formatPrimeOutputPlain` output against the spawn-injection contract from the seed (no markdown headers, no code fences, no decorative ASCII, no leading 'primed at <timestamp>' header) and tighten only what diverges — minimize blast radius on existing search/query --format plain consumers. Suppress the trailing Session Close Protocol reminder when format=plain, since warren handles session framing contextually. (2) Add a `--dry-run` flag that short-circuits content rendering and emits JSON: `{ wouldPrime: [{id, type, domain, tokens}], totalTokens, budgetUsed, budgetTotal }`. Reuse the existing budget/token estimation pipeline (applyBudget + estimateRecordText in src/utils/budget.ts) so dry-run numbers match what real prime would emit, and respect the budget (only list what fits) per the seed's lean-in answer. `--format plain --dry-run` returns the JSON summary (format ignored when dry-running). Both flags compose with existing --domain, --files, --budget.

alternatives.name: New top-level `ml plan` or `ml preview` command
alternatives.rejected_because: prime is the right entry point; format and dry-run are presentation concerns, not new capabilities. Adding a parallel command duplicates filtering/budget logic.

alternatives.name: Make `plain` the default format on non-TTY stdout
alternatives.rejected_because: Per the seed's open-question lean: keep the current default. Explicit `--format plain` is clearer and won't surprise existing pipe consumers (CI scripts, agent runners that capture output).

alternatives.name: Include a content fragment (first ~50 chars per record) in dry-run output
alternatives.rejected_because: Per the seed's lean: keep dry-run lightweight. Warren can fetch full content via `ml show <id>` if richer preview is ever needed; bloating dry-run defeats its purpose as a fast UI feedback path.

alternatives.name: Add a `--summary` flag instead of `--dry-run`
alternatives.rejected_because: `--dry-run` is the conventional name and matches `ml compact --dry-run` / `ml prune --dry-run` precedent in this codebase. Consistency wins.

steps.title: Audit existing `--format plain` output against the spawn-injection contract
steps.description: Run `ml prime --format plain` against the current repo's .mulch/ and capture the output. Verify against the seed's contract: (a) no markdown headers/code fences/decorative ASCII, (b) one record per stanza separated by blank lines, (c) no 'primed at <timestamp>' header, (d) no trailing Session Close Protocol reminder (currently appended unconditionally at src/commands/prime.ts:365). Also exercise `--manifest --format plain` (formatPrimeManifestPlain) for completeness. Document any divergence in a short note attached to this plan. Output of this step is a punch list for step 2 — no code changes here.

steps.title: Tighten `--format plain` rendering to match the spawn-injection contract
steps.description: Address the punch list from step 1. At minimum: skip the `getSessionEndReminder(format)` append in src/commands/prime.ts when format === 'plain' (the same `output += \n\n${getSessionEndReminder(format)}` block runs at lines ~227 for manifest and ~365 for full prime — handle both call sites). If the audit also flags formatDomainExpertisePlain or formatPrimeOutputPlain noise, fix it in src/utils/format.ts. Be conservative: search.ts and query.ts also call these formatters via the global --format flag (src/commands/search.ts:274, src/commands/query.ts:187), so any change must keep their output sensible. Update test/commands/prime.test.ts assertions on plain output if shape changes.

steps.title: Implement `--dry-run` JSON output on `ml prime`
steps.description: Add `.option('--dry-run', 'emit metadata about what would be primed without rendering content')` to the prime command in src/commands/prime.ts. When set, run the full record-loading + filtering + budget pipeline (so wouldPrime reflects what real prime would emit), but emit `{ wouldPrime: [{id, type, domain, tokens}], totalTokens, budgetUsed, budgetTotal }` as JSON to stdout instead of formatted records. Reuse `estimateRecordText` from src/utils/budget.ts for per-record token counts so numbers match real budget enforcement. `budgetTotal` reflects --budget (or DEFAULT_BUDGET); `--no-limit` should set budgetTotal to null and budgetUsed to null. Skip `getSessionEndReminder` and the pre-prime hook output mutation path (dry-run is a preview, not a write — same pattern as `ml prune --dry-run` per the soft-archive-prune precedent). When --dry-run is combined with --format, ignore --format and emit JSON regardless. When combined with --export, write JSON to the file.

steps.title: Add tests covering plain format + dry-run + composition
steps.description: Extend test/commands/prime.test.ts with three test groups: (a) `--format plain` produces clean text — assert no markdown headers ('# '), no code fences ('```'), no Session Close Protocol marker (e.g. '🚨' or 'Session Close Protocol' substring), records appear as expected stanzas; (b) `--dry-run` returns valid JSON with wouldPrime array (each entry has id/type/domain/tokens), totalTokens equal to sum of tokens, budgetUsed equal to totalTokens/budgetTotal, --no-limit produces null budget fields; (c) composition: `--format plain --dry-run` returns JSON (format ignored), `--dry-run --domain <X>` filters to domain X, `--dry-run --files <path>` filters by file relevance, `--dry-run --budget 1000` truncates wouldPrime to fit. Follow the no-mocks convention (mx-53d431) — write real .mulch/expertise/<domain>.jsonl fixtures via mkdtemp and assert on real CLI output.

steps.title: Update docs: CHANGELOG, README, command help text
steps.description: Add an entry under [Unreleased] in CHANGELOG.md (Keep a Changelog format per mx-900a53) describing the new `--dry-run` flag and the plain-format polish for spawn-time injection. Add a short README section under prime usage showing the warren spawn-injection example: `ml prime --domain X --files F --budget N --format plain` and the dry-run preview shape. Update the option help string for `--format plain` in src/cli.ts if the current wording doesn't mention spawn injection. Per mx-6ac234, keep README in sync.

risks: Tightening `formatDomainExpertisePlain` or `formatPrimeOutputPlain` could regress existing `ml search --format plain` and `ml query --format plain` consumers (src/commands/search.ts:274, src/commands/query.ts:187). Mitigation: keep changes additive (suppress trailers in prime.ts call sites rather than mutating shared formatters) wherever possible.

`estimateRecordText` in budget.ts historically only switched over the 6 built-in record types; custom-typed records were unestimated (mx-8662bd). Dry-run must dispatch via the type registry to avoid undercounting tokens for custom types. Verify this is already routed through the registry before relying on it.

Suppressing the Session Close Protocol reminder per format risks fragmenting the cross-tool 'session close' pattern (mx-4d72e9). Document the intent: warren handles session framing in its own dispatch, so the reminder would be redundant and noisy in a system prompt.

Dry-run that respects the budget could surprise users who expect 'show everything that matched my filter'. The seed answers this (lean: respect budget) — surface it in the help text so the contract is explicit.
If --dry-run runs the pre-prime hook, hook side effects (e.g. Slack posts) would fire on every preview. Per soft-archive-prune precedent (mx-a7b234), dry-run should skip hooks entirely.
acceptance: `ml prime --format plain` emits clean text suitable for system-prompt concatenation: no markdown headers, no code fences, no decorative ASCII, no leading 'primed at <timestamp>' header, no trailing Session Close Protocol reminder.
`ml prime --dry-run` emits JSON `{ wouldPrime: [{id, type, domain, tokens}], totalTokens, budgetUsed, budgetTotal }` without rendering record content; numbers match what real prime would emit.
`ml prime --format plain --dry-run` is a valid combination and returns the dry-run JSON summary (format ignored when dry-running).
Both flags compose with existing `--domain`, `--files`, `--budget`, `--no-limit`, `--exclude-domain` flags.
Test coverage in test/commands/prime.test.ts asserts plain shape + dry-run JSON shape + composition; `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` pass.
CHANGELOG.md and README.md updated per project conventions (mx-900a53, mx-6ac234).

outcomeNote: All 5 child steps implemented: plain audit + tightened formatPrimeOutputPlain (preserved formatDomainExpertisePlain for search/query), --dry-run with budget/hook precedence, latent --no-limit bug fixed (Commander parses as options.limit), 9 new tests, README + CHANGELOG updated. 1181 tests pass.

sections.context: V1_PLAN.md §3 stages mulch's path to v1.0.0 across three releases (v0.10 → v0.11 → v1.0.0) because two new concepts (freshness_score formula, note type semantics) need empirical calibration before being baked into the schema freeze. v0.10 is the 'visibility + prime overhaul' release: it adds the measurement surface (ml audit + age-aware status) so teams can see floater %, evidence coverage, rotting domains, and convention bloat; centralizes and softens the close-session prose so agents stop generating ritual filler records (audit finding: 70–80% of conventions are restated module layouts); and overhauls ml prime — the only mulch surface every agent reads, every session — so its content shape stops wasting context and starts presenting the corpus by trust tier and relevance. Schema is unchanged in v0.10; recorded_by, freshness_score, and the note type ship in v0.11 once threshold defaults have a quarter of real-corpus data behind them. Two design constraints from V1_PLAN: (a) the prime overhaul must ship as one coherent change in v0.10 because fragmenting it produces a v0.10 worse than v0.9 (e.g. conditional close without lead-with-contract leaves agents unsure what's expected of them), and (b) hardcoding seeds-and-gh as first-class trackers while linear/bead stay second-class would be a v1.0 contract bug, so the active-work resolver chain (§4.5) ships in v0.10 to back the prime context-scope feature and lay the groundwork for v0.11's auto-link-on-record. Mid-cycle expansion (rev 2): four curation-surface gaps surfaced while V1_PLAN was being lived against the actual codebase — `ml prune` only acts on stale records (cannot manually archive), `ml compact` mechanically concatenates record bodies and silently deletes the originals, archived records lose the *why* of their archival, and `ml prune --dry-run` summary requires `--explain` to show record IDs (inverts the safety contract). These four absorbed into v0.10 because they share the soft-archive substrate already shipped in v0.9 (R-05a) and are the missing user-facing surface that makes the v0.10 visibility surfaces (`ml audit`, age-aware status) actionable: surfacing bloat without a clean way to act on it leaves the corpus in the same shape the audit identified. The semantic-summarization half of the compact overhaul ships behind a `pre-compact` hook so v0.10 picks up summarization without bundling an LLM dependency. `ml curate` (cluster-by-bloat surface, subsumes mulch-e2bd) is filed separately as mulch-7303 and deferred to v0.11 — it composes the v0.10 primitives once they are stable. The v0.10 schema-unchanged constraint from V1_PLAN §3 is preserved: `archive_reason` lands only on archived records (`.mulch/archive/<domain>.jsonl`), which V1_PLAN §2 explicitly excludes from the v1.0 frozen live `BaseRecord` envelope.
sections.approach: Eight PR-sized steps that map to the four v0.10 deliverables from V1_PLAN.md §3. Stage them so the foundational primitives land first (audit, status age, resolver chain, close-session prose centralization) and the prime overhaul layers on top in three coherent slices (lead-with-contract + manifest default, context-scope default, trust-tier ranking + why-surfaced suffix). The split is by review-surface size, not by §-section in V1_PLAN: audit's full flag surface (--ci/--suggest/--domain) ships as one PR because the flags share an output formatter; the prime overhaul splits at the points where the rendering pipeline changes shape, not where V1_PLAN bullets break. Reuse existing primitives wherever possible: readExpertiseFile/applyBudget/filterByContext for audit and prime, computeConfirmationScore (★N) for trust-tier ranking, prime.default_mode config (already plumbed) for the manifest flip, getSessionEndReminder for the central prose helper. The pre-prime 'consider --manifest' stderr warning gets retired when the default flips — the tool stops being aware-but-wrong. Tests use the existing 'no mocks, real filesystems with mkdtemp' convention (mx-53d431); CLI VERSION (src/cli.ts + package.json must move together per mx-5ab2bc) and CHANGELOG/README (mx-900a53, mx-6ac234) move in the final release step. The four added curation steps (rev 2) thread off the existing soft-archive util (src/utils/archive.ts:60) and the existing prune signal payload (DemotionExplain in prune.ts): archive_reason is the foundational schema-cheap addition that the other three all consume; ml archive reuses archiveRecords + withFileLock; the granular dry-run print is a 40-line patch in prune.ts's per-domain print loop; the compact overhaul reuses the existing runHooks infrastructure (mulch-55b1 R-02) for the new `pre-compact` event and redirects the deletion path through archiveRecords with reason="compacted". Sequencing: archive_reason ships first as the schema prerequisite; ml archive, dry-run granular, and compact overhaul ship in parallel after; release step waits for all four. Tests follow the same real-filesystem-with-mkdtemp pattern (mx-53d431) as the existing v0.10 steps.

alternatives.name: Ship audit and status in v0.10, defer prime overhaul to v0.11
alternatives.rejected_because: V1_PLAN.md §5.2 is explicit: the prime overhaul is the single highest-leverage behavioral change in the whole v1.0 plan and has no schema implications. Deferring it wastes a release cycle on visibility without acting on what visibility reveals. The audit will surface bad conventions and prime will continue to broadcast them at every session start.

alternatives.name: Fragment the prime overhaul across v0.10 and v0.11
alternatives.rejected_because: V1_PLAN.md §5.2 explicitly forbids this: 'fragmenting it creates a v0.10 that is worse than v0.9 (e.g. conditional close without lead-with-contract leaves agents unsure what is expected of them).' The six sub-changes are coherent because they reshape the *same* output; splitting them produces a half-overhauled prime that is more confusing than the current one.

alternatives.name: Hardcode seeds + gh in prime context-scope; defer linear/bead resolvers to v0.11
alternatives.rejected_because: V1_PLAN.md §4.5 calls this out as a v1.0 contract bug: the record schema already supports seeds/gh/linear/bead symmetrically (src/schemas/record.ts:16-25), so any first-class/second-class split at the resolver layer leaks asymmetry into a frozen contract. The resolver chain pattern is ~20 lines per resolver and ships once.

alternatives.name: Port mulch-audit.py to TS with --ci as a follow-up issue
alternatives.rejected_because: --ci is the ROI multiplier — without it audit is observational, not actionable. V1_PLAN.md §4.2 lists --ci/--suggest/--domain as ship-together. Splitting the CLI surface forces a second round of help-text/test/doc churn for ~30% more line count.

alternatives.name: Build a parallel confirmations[]/challenges[] system instead of reusing outcomes[] for trust-tier ranking
alternatives.rejected_because: V1_PLAN.md §4.3: '20% of the work is done by the existing primitives. Building parallel arrays would split the truth across two fields, double the merge logic in warren's mulch_merge, and force every consumer to read both.' The ★N markers in compactMeta (src/utils/format-helpers.ts:79) and computeConfirmationScore in scoring.ts:58 already drive ranking; v0.10's trust-tier sort wires those into prime's ordering.

alternatives.name: Remove the 🚨 emoji from the close-session prompt as part of the reframe
alternatives.rejected_because: V1_PLAN.md §5.2 (point 6): the 🚨 marker functions as a memory anchor for agents whose context has filled with file edits since session start, raising the odds the close protocol gets re-read. The problem the audit identified is the *demand shape* of the prose, not the visual marker. Reframe the body to conditional; keep the 🚨.

alternatives.name: Defer ml compact overhaul to v1.0; v0.10 ships archive-integration only
alternatives.rejected_because: The semantic-summarization piece does not require an LLM dependency in v0.10 — the `pre-compact` hook is provider-neutral by design (any script can return a JSON replacement body), so v0.10 absorbs the full overhaul without bundling LLM code. Splitting archive-integration into v0.10 and semantic-summarization into v1.0 would force a second PR cycle for the same compact.ts touch points and double-document the compact contract. The hook absent → mechanical merge fallback preserves back-compat for users who do not configure a summarizer.

alternatives.name: Ship ml curate in v0.10 (heuristic clustering only); defer semantic to v0.11
alternatives.rejected_because: Curate composes the four other curation surfaces (audit, archive, archive_reason, compact) — building it in v0.10 means iterating on the composition while the underlying primitives are still stabilizing. v0.11 lets curate land against settled v0.10 surfaces and pick up the v0.11 schema additions (`recorded_by`, `freshness_score`) at the same time, which give curate richer recommendations. mulch-7303 tracks the v0.11 work and subsumes mulch-e2bd's semantic-grouping stub.

steps.title: ml audit command — full surface (port mulch-audit.py to TS + --ci + --suggest + --domain + config thresholds)
steps.description: Port /tmp/mulch-audit.py (199 lines) to src/commands/audit.ts. Reuse readExpertiseFile() (type-aware, alias-resolving). Ship four invocations: bare `ml audit` (human-readable, exit 0 always), `ml audit --ci` (exit 1 on FAIL bands, JSON output), `ml audit --suggest` (record-ID-level remediation: 'run ml prune --ids mx-abc,mx-def'), `ml audit --domain <name>` (scope to one domain). Add `audit.thresholds: { evidence_coverage, floater_max, rule_density_min, max_records_per_domain, max_stale }` to MulchConfig schema (src/schemas/config.ts) and DEFAULT_CONFIG backfill (src/utils/config.ts). v1.0-aimed defaults per V1_PLAN §4.2: evidence_coverage ≥ 0.5 (relaxed from script's 0.7), floater_max ≤ 0.2, rule_density_min ≥ 0.25 (audit found NO repo passing 0.4; ship 0.25 as the v1.0 floor with override). Document the rule-density regex (the same RULE_SIGNALS tuple from the Python prototype) in the command help — V1_PLAN §4.2 accepts the metric is approximate. --by-author flag is stubbed out (lands when recorded_by lands in v0.11). Register in src/cli.ts.

steps.title: Age-aware ml status — surface domain age range and rotting flag
steps.description: Extend formatStatusOutput in src/utils/format.ts (lines 469-501) so each domain line shows first-recorded → last-recorded range (mirrors the Python prototype's 'domain age' section, lines 175-183) and a rotting flag when the last write is older than config.classification_defaults.shelf_life.observational. Update src/commands/status.ts to compute the age range from readExpertiseFile results (recorded_at is on BaseRecord). JSON mode adds {first_recorded_at, last_recorded_at, rotting: boolean} to each domain entry. Tests assert age formatting and the rotting flag boundary.

steps.title: Active-work resolver chain (src/utils/active-work.ts) — seeds/gh/linear/bead symmetric resolvers
steps.description: New file src/utils/active-work.ts exports `resolveActiveWork(cwd?: string): Promise<{ seeds?: string, gh?: string, linear?: string, bead?: string, branch?: string }>`. Implementation: four ~20-line resolvers (seeds via `sd ready --mine` and current-branch parsing; gh via `gh issue list --assignee @me --state open` with conservative 'auto-link only on exactly one match' behavior per V1_PLAN §7; linear and bead stubs that return empty until consumers exist). Runner: first non-empty result per tracker wins; explicit --evidence-* flags always override at call sites. Stderr warning when exactly-one-match policy filters out 2+ candidates so wrong links self-correct. Foundation for prime context-scope (slice 2) and v0.11's auto-link-on-record (V1_PLAN §4.1 third bullet). No CLI command surface yet — pure utility. Tests stub the tracker binaries via PATH manipulation (no mocks per mx-53d431).

steps.title: Centralize and reframe close-session prose — single helper, conditional language, preserve 🚨
steps.description: Today the 'before you finish' prose lives in four places: src/commands/setup.ts CURSOR_RULE_CONTENT (lines 298-331), src/commands/setup.ts CODEX_SECTION (lines 387-411), src/commands/onboard.ts (lines 61-73), and src/utils/format.ts:getSessionEndReminder (lines 426-467). V1_PLAN §5.1: 'centralize the prose in a single helper so the three recipes stay in sync.' Extract one helper (likely in src/utils/format.ts) that emits per-format variants (xml/plain/markdown) of the new conditional prose. New body per V1_PLAN §5.2 (point 6): '🚨 Before closing this session: if you discovered a non-obvious convention, hit a real failure, or made a decision someone would otherwise re-derive, run ml record. If the session was routine, close without recording — ml audit surfaces anything stale.' Preserve the 🚨 prefix in markdown/plain (functions as a memory anchor per V1_PLAN §5.2). Cursor + Codex recipes + onboard all import the helper. Tests cover all four call sites and assert the conditional verb structure ('if you discovered…') replaces the ritual demand ('you MUST run this checklist').

steps.title: ml prime overhaul, slice 1: lead with the contract + flip default to manifest at >100 records or >5 domains
steps.description: Two coupled changes at the top of the prime output (V1_PLAN §5.2 points 1 and 2). (a) Lead with the contract: new top-of-output block pulled from MulchConfig listing active write-side gates (`require_because: on/off`, `audit.thresholds`, allowed types per domain via DomainConfig, `note` exclusion rules — though `note` itself lands in v0.11; emit a placeholder section that becomes populated then). Tells the agent what's expected before showing them what to know. Borrows the rules-first ordering from sd prime per V1_PLAN. (b) Manifest default at the threshold: when config.prime.default_mode is unset (or `auto`), apply heuristic — manifest if total records >100 OR domain count >5; otherwise full. Add `auto` as a third PrimeMode in src/schemas/config.ts. Retire the pre-prime 'consider --manifest' stderr warning (src/utils/config.ts and any hook config) — the tool stops being aware-but-wrong because the default *is* the right thing. `ml prime --full` continues to force full mode. CHANGELOG flags this as a breaking output-shape change for any consumers parsing prime output. Tests: contract block presence + manifest threshold boundaries (100/5).

steps.title: ml prime overhaul, slice 2: context-scope as default for full mode
steps.description: V1_PLAN §5.2 point 3. When --full is active (forced or via small corpus), auto-detect scope: getChangedFiles() from src/utils/git.ts for git-status changed/untracked files (already exists), current branch name, in-progress trackers via resolveActiveWork() from the resolver-chain step. Filter the records-to-load set to those matching that scope (reuse filterByContext from src/utils/git.ts:filterByContext). New flag `--all` opts out for the unfiltered dump; otherwise emit a stderr line per V1_PLAN §5.2 risk-mitigation: `scoped to N of M records based on git status; --all for full corpus`. Tests: scope detection produces expected record subsets; --all preserves the existing full-dump behavior; the stderr line fires only in default (non --all) full mode.

steps.title: ml prime overhaul, slice 3: trust-tier ranking within scope + per-record 'why surfaced' suffix
steps.description: Two coupled rendering-layer changes (V1_PLAN §5.2 points 4 and 5). (a) Trust-tier ranking: within the surfaced set, sort by tier — ★-confirmed (computeConfirmationScore > 0, src/utils/scoring.ts:58) → foundational → tactical → observational. Within a tier, order by relevance to current scope (file-anchor overlap count, then recency). Replaces today's per-domain alphabetical dump. Config knob `prime.tier_weights: { star_bonus, foundational, tactical, observational }` overrides per V1_PLAN §5.2 risks. (b) Per-record 'why surfaced' suffix: one short tag appended after the existing compactMeta (src/utils/format-helpers.ts:79) — `(matches src/foo.ts)`, `(★3 in this domain)`, `(in_progress: seed-abc)`, `(recently authored: 2d ago)`. Computed atop the existing scoring/relevance pipeline; one tag per record (the most salient). Tests: tier ordering invariant (★-confirmed always precedes plain foundational of the same age), suffix surfaces the dominant reason, tier_weights override changes ranking.

steps.title: archive_reason field on archived records — auditable archive metadata
steps.description: Add optional `archive_reason: string` field to archived records (lives only on .mulch/archive/<domain>.jsonl rows; not on the v1.0-frozen live BaseRecord envelope, preserving V1_PLAN §3 'schema unchanged in v0.10'). Convention values: "stale" | "superseded" | "anchor_decay" | "manual" | "compacted" — free-text suffix permitted ("manual: wrong domain"). Update archiveRecords() in src/utils/archive.ts:60 to take a required `reason` parameter; thread through every caller — prune.ts staleness path → "stale", supersession-bottom → "superseded", anchor-decay-bottom → "anchor_decay". formatArchivedSection in src/commands/search.ts (line 26) prints the reason in `[ARCHIVED <date> <reason>] mx-... [type] ...`. ml restore success message includes the reason. Existing archive files without the field continue to load (back-compat). Tests in test/utils/archive.test.ts cover round-trip and missing-field back-compat. Schema-cheap, auditability-rich; foundational for the three downstream steps. Tracking seed: mulch-b41a.

steps.title: ml archive command — direct archive without prune (with --reason)
steps.description: New src/commands/archive.ts registered in src/cli.ts. Args: `<domain> [id]` plus `--records <a,b,c>` for bulk (matching ml delete ergonomics from seeds-86c7), `--reason <text>` (required), `--dry-run` (preview). Reuses archiveRecords() + withFileLock(); removes records from live JSONL inside the same lock as the archive write so concurrent writers stay consistent. Populates archive_reason as "manual: <user text>" via the field added in the prerequisite step. JSON output: `{ success, command: "archive", domain, archived: [{id, type, reason}] }`. Closes the soft-archive UX gap: prune handles bulk decay; ml archive handles targeted intent. Symmetric to existing ml restore. Tests in test/commands/archive.test.ts mirror the ml restore test patterns (real filesystem, mkdtemp, no mocks per mx-53d431). ml restore round-trip on a manually-archived record asserted as regression test. Tracking seed: mulch-d563.

steps.title: ml prune --dry-run granular summary by default — drop --explain barrier for record IDs
steps.description: Patch prune.ts ~lines 448-490 (the per-domain print loop) so dry-run prints one line per archived/demoted record by default: `mx-abc123 [pattern]: foundational → archived (anchor_decay)`. Reason label comes from the archive_reason values added in the prerequisite step. Today dry-run prints only one line per domain ("Would archive 5 of 70 records") which inverts the safety contract: the operator drops --dry-run without seeing which records changed. --explain keeps its current responsibility (per-record anchor breakdown: valid_fraction, broken anchor list). --quiet suppresses the per-record list (totals only). --json mode unchanged (already structured). Tests in test/commands/prune.test.ts cover the new default output and back-compat for --explain / --quiet / --json. Tracking seed: mulch-5ce3.

steps.title: ml compact overhaul — pre-compact hook for semantic summary + archive originals instead of delete
steps.description: Two-part overhaul in src/commands/compact.ts. (a) Archive integration: handleAuto and handleApply route the source records through archiveRecords(domain, recordsToCompact, now, "compacted") under the same lock instead of dropping them via updatedRecords.filter. The replacement record still carries supersedes: [old_ids]; ml restore mx-old brings any single source back if compaction was wrong. (b) pre-compact lifecycle hook: new event registered in src/utils/hooks.ts event list. Payload: `{ domain, type, records: [<full record objects>] }` on stdin; mutable like pre-record / pre-prime — hook may print `{ replacement: <RecordShape> }` on stdout to override the mechanical concat/keep_latest/merge_outcomes merge. Hook absent or empty stdout → mechanical merge runs as today (back-compat). Replacement is AJV-validated before write; malformed shape rejected per existing hook payload validators. Doctor adds informational `compact-summarizer` check noting whether pre-compact is registered. Example reference hook ships in examples/hooks/llm-summarize.sh — not bundled, no LLM dependency in mulch core; the hook surface is provider-neutral. ml restore round-trip on a compacted-archived record asserted as regression test. Tracking seed: mulch-184b.

steps.title: Release: bump VERSION to 0.10.0, update CHANGELOG and README
steps.description: Bump src/cli.ts VERSION export and package.json version to 0.10.0 (per convention mx-5ab2bc: must move together). Add CHANGELOG.md entry per Keep a Changelog format (convention mx-900a53) — flag the prime-default flip as a behavioral breaking change, list the new audit/status surface, the close-session prose reframe, and the resolver chain as additive. Update README.md to describe `ml audit` and the new prime defaults (convention mx-6ac234). Run bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck (per CLAUDE.md). No git tag in this seed — release tag is a separate human-driven step. CHANGELOG also lists the curation absorption: ml archive (new command), archive_reason field on archived records, ml prune --dry-run granular default summary, ml compact overhaul (archive originals + pre-compact hook). Note the absorption explicitly in the release notes since V1_PLAN.md framed v0.10 as visibility-only — readers comparing the plan to the release should see the rationale (curation surfaces make the visibility surfaces actionable; see plan rev 2 for the alternatives discussion).

risks: Detection-based prime context-scope can mis-scope when the agent's working set diverges from git status (e.g. exploration before edits). Mitigation per V1_PLAN §5.2: --all escape hatch + stderr line reporting the scoped/total ratio so the agent sees what got dropped.

Trust-tier ranking may demote unconfirmed-but-genuinely-load-bearing foundational records (records that nobody has run `ml confirm` against because that command doesn't ship until v0.11). Mitigation: prime.tier_weights config override; ship default weights conservative enough that foundational still outranks tactical even with no stars.

Conditional close-session prompt may suppress true-positive records, not just filler. Mitigation per V1_PLAN §5.2: watch post-v0.10 `ml audit` metrics; if record volume drops without quality rising (rule-density or evidence-coverage flat), the prompt over-corrected and the body needs sharpening in v0.10.x patch.

Manifest-as-default flips output shape; downstream consumers parsing prime output (warren, tooling) may break. Mitigation: CHANGELOG flags the breaking change; existing `pre-prime` 'consider --manifest' warning in current codebase has been signaling this direction for a release already; consumers can pin `--full --all` for the legacy shape.

Active-work resolver chain may auto-link wrong issues when multiple candidates match. V1_PLAN §7: ship resolvers with conservative behavior (auto-link only on exactly one match) and stamp a stderr line so wrong links self-correct via explicit --evidence-* flags.

audit.thresholds defaults will be wrong for some corpus shapes (V1_PLAN §7) — reference-doc domains are legitimately convention-heavy and evidence-light. Mitigation: ship per-domain threshold overrides in the config block and --ignore-domains in the audit CLI surface; accept that the first quarter of usage will surface tuning needs.

Centralized close-session helper changes the prose surface; existing CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / Cursor rule files installed by previous `ml setup` runs will go stale. The setup recipes' check() methods will flag mismatch (existing behavior); users re-run setup to pick up the new prose. Acceptable per the same upgrade path that existed for prior recipe content changes.

Rule-density regex in audit over-counts Bun-isms ('avoid process.exit') as conventions and under-counts 'we …' phrasings (V1_PLAN §4.2). Documented limitation; metric is approximate. Threshold defaults assume this skew.

Mid-cycle scope expansion (rev 2 adds four curation steps to a release V1_PLAN framed as visibility-only) risks pushing the v0.10 timeline. Mitigation: archive_reason is a schema-cheap prerequisite (~30-line PR); the other three thread off existing primitives (archiveRecords, withFileLock, runHooks) and ship as small parallel PRs. If the compact overhaul slips, archive-integration alone (without the pre-compact hook) is the minimum viable subset and can ship while the hook surface follows in v0.10.x.

pre-compact hook may produce replacement bodies that fail AJV validation in subtle ways (missing required fields for the type). Mitigation: replacement is validated against the type registry before write; failure surfaces a copy-paste retry hint matching the existing record-validation pattern; hook script can iterate before re-running compact.

archive_reason on archives only (not live records) is mildly asymmetric — readers may expect symmetry with status: "archived" which also lives only on archived rows. Mitigation: documented in CLAUDE.md alongside the existing soft-archive contract; consistent precedent (archived_at, status are also archive-only).
acceptance: `ml audit` runs against the mulch corpus and reproduces the Python prototype's PASS/WARN/FAIL signals (within tolerance for the relaxed v1.0 thresholds: evidence_coverage ≥ 0.5, rule_density_min ≥ 0.25).
`ml audit --ci` exits 1 when any threshold is in the FAIL band and emits JSON; exits 0 otherwise.
`ml audit --suggest` outputs specific record IDs grouped by remediation action (archive / revise / attribute) as copy-paste-able `ml prune --ids …` and `ml edit <id> …` commands.
`ml audit --domain <name>` scopes all metrics to one domain; `audit.thresholds` config block overrides every default; per-domain overrides supported.
`ml status` shows first-recorded → last-recorded range per domain and flags rotting domains (no write within observational shelf life).

Close-session prose comes from a single helper used by Cursor recipe, Codex recipe, `ml onboard`, and prime footer; phrasing is conditional (`if you discovered …`) not ritualistic (`you MUST run this checklist`); the 🚨 marker is preserved in markdown/plain variants.
`ml prime` default output leads with the project's mulch contract block (active write-side gates from config) before any record content.
`ml prime` default mode flips to manifest at the >100-records or >5-domains threshold; the pre-prime 'consider --manifest' stderr warning is removed because the default *is* the right thing.
`ml prime --full` context-scopes by default using git status + branch + active-work resolver; `--all` opts out and emits the full corpus.
Within the surfaced set, records are ordered by trust tier (★-confirmed → foundational → tactical → observational); `prime.tier_weights` config overrides the default weights.
Every surfaced record carries a 'why surfaced now' suffix (one of: file match, ★ count, in-progress tracker, recent authorship).

`src/utils/active-work.ts` returns first-non-empty matches across seeds/gh/linear/bead resolvers; conservative one-match-only auto-link with stderr warning on multi-match; explicit --evidence-* flags always override.

`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` pass; test/commands/audit.test.ts, test/commands/status.test.ts, test/commands/prime.test.ts, test/utils/active-work.test.ts, test/utils/format.test.ts all cover the new surfaces with real-filesystem fixtures (no mocks per mx-53d431).

CLI VERSION in src/cli.ts and package.json bumped together to 0.10.0 (mx-5ab2bc); CHANGELOG.md entry follows Keep a Changelog format (mx-900a53) and flags the prime-default flip as a behavioral breaking change; README.md updated (mx-6ac234).

`archive_reason` lands on every archived record written by prune ("stale", "superseded", "anchor_decay"), ml archive ("manual: <text>"), and ml compact ("compacted"); archive files written before this seed continue to load without the field.

`ml archive <domain> <id> --reason "..."` moves a record from live to archive in one lock acquisition; `--records <a,b,c>` bulk-archives; `--dry-run` previews; ml restore round-trips a manually-archived record back to live.

`ml prune --dry-run` default output prints one line per affected record (id, type, tier transition, reason) without --explain; --explain still adds the per-record anchor breakdown; --quiet collapses to totals only; --json unchanged.

`ml compact --apply` and `ml compact --auto` archive removed records to .mulch/archive/<domain>.jsonl with archive_reason="compacted" instead of silently dropping them; ml restore round-trips a compacted-archived record back to live.

A registered `pre-compact` hook returning `{ replacement: <RecordShape> }` overrides the mechanical merge body; absent hook → mechanical merge unchanged (back-compat for existing compact tests); malformed hook output rejected per the existing runHooks contract; ml doctor surfaces `compact-summarizer: configured | not configured (mechanical merge in use)`.

sections.context: Mulch integration with pi (the coding-agent harness becoming the daily-driver runtime) is conventional today: CLAUDE.md tells the LLM to run `ml prime` and `ml record`, and we hope it remembers. It drops `ml record` mid-session, fails to record failures after debug detours, and re-primes when reloaded. A pi extension hard-wires these rituals into pi lifecycle events — session_start prime, tool_call scope-load, agent_end record-nudge, plus structured record_expertise/query_expertise tools so the LLM stops escaping into bash for what should be a one-call interaction. Investigation in os-eco/docs/pi-extensions-investigation.md §1; pi extension API surface validated against /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent@0.74.0.
sections.approach: Ship inside @os-eco/mulch-cli (Option A from the investigation doc) — adds extensions/pi/index.ts + supporting lib files, plus a pi manifest and peerDependencies block in package.json. The tool surface (record_expertise) is registered dynamically inside session_start from the in-process type-registry (src/registry/type-registry.ts), so custom types like release_decision and flake_symptom and per-domain allowed_types rules are always reflected accurately — no drift against the running ml record contract. Config knobs live under a pi.* namespace inside mulch.config.yaml (one source of truth, atomic writes, schema validation inherited). Onboarding marker stays on the existing <!-- mulch-onboard:v<ver> --> shape with a :pi suffix when the pi recipe is installed, so existing detection logic doubles as install-state detection.

alternatives.name: Hard-code the six built-in record-type schemas in the extension
alternatives.rejected_because: Drifts when projects declare custom_types; release_decision/flake_symptom would silently fail validation against ml record.

alternatives.name: Block on mulch-e3b5 (ml record schema subcommand) before shipping
alternatives.rejected_because: In-tree dynamic registration via type-registry imports is strictly cleaner since the extension lives in the same tarball — same answer, no extra dependency. mulch-e3b5 still ships afterwards for out-of-tree consumers.

alternatives.name: Sibling package via bun workspaces (Option B from investigation doc)
alternatives.rejected_because: Version-locking with the CLI is the whole point. One tarball, one version, can't drift. Option B is reserved for warren in the doc.

alternatives.name: New <!-- pi-onboard-v --> marker parallel to <!-- mulch-onboard -->
alternatives.rejected_because: Doubles detection logic and forces a migration for anyone with the current marker. Suffix variant on the existing marker is a one-line change to getVersionMarker() and reuses isSnippetCurrent comparison.

alternatives.name: Shadow config in .pi/mulch.json
alternatives.rejected_because: Would lose file-locking, schema validation, and multi-agent concurrency safety that mulch.config.yaml already provides. pi.* namespace in the existing config inherits all of it.

steps.title: Foundation: extension skeleton, peer deps, pi.* config block
steps.description: Create extensions/pi/{index.ts,lib/mulch-runner.ts} skeleton — index.ts exports default factory, mulch-runner.ts wraps Bun.spawn for `ml` subprocess calls threading ctx.cwd + ctx.signal + JSON parsing. Add `pi` field to package.json pointing at ./extensions/pi/index.ts, add peerDependencies (@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent ^0.74.0, @earendil-works/pi-ai *, typebox *) with peerDependenciesMeta optional:true for all three, mirror in devDependencies so typecheck and tests resolve. Add `pi` block to src/schemas/config.ts MulchConfig type and src/schemas/config-schema.ts JSON schema (auto_prime: bool default true, scope_load: {enabled: bool true, budget: int 2000, debounce_ms: int 300}, learn_widget: {enabled: bool true}). Extensions/pi/ must compile under tsc --noEmit. No behavior wired yet — this is shape only.

steps.title: Auto-prime: session_start cache then before_agent_start systemPrompt inject
steps.description: extensions/pi/lib/prime.ts: on session_start (any reason — startup/reload/new/resume/fork), shell `ml prime --json` via mulch-runner (respecting prime.default_mode from config), cache the rendered markdown in extension-module scope keyed by ctx.cwd. On before_agent_start, return { systemPrompt: event.systemPrompt + '\n\n' + cachedPrime } — append, never replace (chains across extensions). Gated by config pi.auto_prime. Doc's spike-to-do-first; everything else builds on this pattern.

steps.title: Scope-load on tool_call: debounced ml prime --files plus persistence
steps.description: extensions/pi/lib/scope-load.ts: on tool_call, use isToolCallEventType to narrow read/edit/write, extract path from event.input. Maintain per-session primedPaths: Set<string>; persist via pi.appendEntry('mulch-primed', { paths }) on update, restore on session_start by walking ctx.sessionManager.getEntries() for entry.type === 'custom' && entry.customType === 'mulch-primed'. Debounce via configurable window (pi.scope_load.debounce_ms, default 300ms): collapse rapid tool_calls touching different paths into one `ml prime --files <p1> <p2> ... --json --budget N` call. Deliver the rendered records via pi.sendMessage({ customType: 'mulch-scope-load', content, display: false }, { deliverAs: 'steer' }). Gated by pi.scope_load.enabled. Survives /reload.

steps.title: Custom tools: record_expertise + query_expertise (dynamic from type-registry)
steps.description: extensions/pi/lib/tools/{record-expertise.ts,query-expertise.ts} + lib/schema.ts. In session_start (after foundation registry is initialized via initRegistryFromConfig(ctx.cwd)), iterate listTypes() and config.domains to generate a TypeBox discriminated-union schema for record_expertise (StringEnum for the `type` field per pi-ai requirement). Client-side guard in execute() rejects domain/type mismatches against config.domains[d].allowed_types with a structured error pointing to allowedTypes — saves an LLM round-trip on validation. promptGuidelines bullets name the tool explicitly per pi docs (no 'use this tool when...'). query_expertise wraps `ml search` / `ml prime --files`. Both shell out via mulch-runner threading ctx.cwd + ctx.signal. No tests yet — those land in step 7.

steps.title: Commands + agent_end learn-nudge widget
steps.description: extensions/pi/lib/commands/{ml-prime.ts,ml-status.ts,ml-doctor.ts}: register /ml:prime [domain?] /ml:status /ml:doctor wrapping the corresponding ml commands, render output via ctx.ui (gated by ctx.hasUI for dialog calls). /ml:prime gets getArgumentCompletions enumerating domains from .mulch/mulch.config.yaml. extensions/pi/lib/learn-nudge.ts: on agent_end, shell `ml learn --json`, render ctx.ui.setWidget('mulch-learn', [...lines]) when suggestedDomains is non-empty. Clear widget on next agent_start. Gated by pi.learn_widget.enabled. Non-blocking — no LLM round-trip.

steps.title: Setup recipe + pi-aware onboarding marker
steps.description: Add `pi` recipe to src/commands/setup.ts alongside claude/cursor/codex: install() writes/merges .pi/settings.json (project-local) adding extensions: ['./extensions/pi/index.ts'] (or packages: ['npm:@os-eco/mulch-cli'] when installed globally); check() returns installed/outdated/not_installed; remove() reverses. Idempotent — every action is a no-op if state already matches. After install/remove, call runOnboard() to refresh the CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md snippet. In src/commands/onboard.ts: getVersionMarker() now takes a piInstalled flag; emits <!-- mulch-onboard:v<ver>:pi --> when true, falls back to existing shape when false. buildSnippet() branches body content on the same flag — pi-aware variant is the short 'expertise auto-loads via pi-mulch' prose; pi-absent stays as-is. Detection: fileExists('.pi/settings.json') && parsed JSON references @os-eco/mulch-cli or ./extensions/pi. isSnippetCurrent comparison gains the suffix automatically.

steps.title: Tests + README
steps.description: test/extensions/pi/: mock ExtensionAPI harness exposing pi.registerTool, pi.registerCommand, pi.on, pi.sendMessage, pi.appendEntry — captures registrations and emitted events for assertions, mirroring the no-mocks-but-mock-pi convention. Test coverage per behavior step: auto-prime caches and appends, scope-load dedupes and persists across simulated session_start restore, record_expertise validates against type-registry including custom types, per-domain guard returns structured error on mismatch, /ml:* commands shell the right subprocesses, learn-widget renders on suggestedDomains, setup recipe is idempotent across re-runs. extensions/pi/README.md: one-page install + config + commands + tools reference; matches the dynamic-tools.ts example README convention. bun test green, bun run typecheck green, bun run lint green.

risks: Pi extension API drift: pinned to ^0.74.0; minor bumps may add or remove hook fields. Watch for breaking changes in session_start event shape, registerTool definition, or sendMessage deliverAs modes.
peerDependenciesMeta optional behavior: npm CLI-only users (npm install -g @os-eco/mulch-cli) must not see peer-dep warnings. Verify with a clean install before merging step 1.

Type-registry import coupling: extensions/pi/lib/schema.ts imports src/registry/type-registry.ts and src/utils/config.ts. Fine while in-tree, but future extraction (e.g. @os-eco/mulch-schema package) would break the import path. Accepted tradeoff; flag if extraction is planned.

Per-domain rule complexity: this project's ecosystem and orchestration domains require evidence as a top-level field. record_expertise's client-side guard must surface required_fields too, not just allowed_types, or the LLM gets an opaque AJV rejection.

Budget tuning: scope_load.budget=2000 default is a guess. May need to tune downward if tool_call traffic floods the message stream, or upward if records get truncated mid-record. Make sure pi.scope_load.budget is read on every call, not cached at extension load, so config edits take effect without restart.

Dual-source priming: a project with both the pi extension AND the CLAUDE.md prose telling the LLM to run ml prime will prime twice. The marker suffix variant in step 6 mitigates this for projects on the current onboarding snippet, but projects with hand-edited onboarding text will still double-prime. Acceptable for v1; document in README.
acceptance: `pi -e ./extensions/pi/index.ts` loads cleanly in this repo with no errors
`ctx.getSystemPrompt()` after session_start contains primed mulch records when pi.auto_prime is true
Editing a file inside a session emits a `mulch-scope-load` steer message containing records relevant to that file's path
primedPaths set survives `/reload` (verifiable by editing the same file twice across reload — second edit should not re-prime)
`record_expertise` tool is registered, visible in pi tool list, accepts the project's custom types (release_decision, flake_symptom)
`record_expertise` with domain=architecture and type=convention returns a structured error citing allowed_types=[decision, pattern, reference, guide, release_decision]
`/ml:prime cli` re-injects the cli domain via sendMessage(steer)
`/ml:prime <tab>` autocompletes to declared domain names
After an agent_end where ml learn detects changed files, ctx.ui shows a widget with 'Record: <domain>/<type>?' lines
`ml setup pi` writes .pi/settings.json idempotently, updates the CLAUDE.md marker to `:pi` suffix, and `ml setup pi --check` returns installed/up_to_date
`ml setup pi --remove` reverses both changes
Snippet body in CLAUDE.md is the short pi-aware variant when pi is installed; full prose when not
bun test, bun run typecheck, bun run lint all green
extensions/pi/README.md exists with install, config, commands, and tools sections
